ucs.title: [LLM] LuminousMoth DLL side-loading via signed Zoom/Office binary from non-install path
ucs.description: Hunts LuminousMoth's signature execution chain — a legitimately-signed loader from the Zoom or Microsoft Office family running from %TEMP%, %PUBLIC%, ProgramData or a removable-drive path while side-loading a co-resident malicious DLL such as version.dll, wwlib.dll or winnsi.dll. This exact loader+sideload pairing was documented by Kaspersky GReAT and ESET against SE Asian government targets (Myanmar/Philippines/Thailand) in 2020-2022 and overlaps with the sister cluster Mustang Panda / TA416 / Bronze President / Earth Preta.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=false count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name="Zoom.exe" OR Processes.process_name="ZoomLauncher.exe" OR Processes.process_name="ZoomVideoApp.exe" OR Processes.process_name="winword.exe" OR Processes.process_name="OneNote.exe" OR Processes.process_name="PotPlayerMini.exe" OR Processes.process_name="mqsvc.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process_hash Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where NOT match(process_path,"(?i)Program Files\\\\Zoom") AND NOT match(process_path,"(?i)Program Files \\(x86\\)\\\\Zoom") AND NOT match(process_path,"(?i)AppData\\\\Roaming\\\\Zoom") AND NOT match(process_path,"(?i)Microsoft Office") AND NOT match(process_path,"(?i)WindowsApps") | join type=inner dest [ | tstats summariesonly=false count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_name="version.dll" OR Filesystem.file_name="wwlib.dll" OR Filesystem.file_name="winnsi.dll" OR Filesystem.file_name="mfc100u.dll" OR Filesystem.file_name="libcef.dll") AND Filesystem.action="created" by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rename file_path as sideload_path file_name as sideload_dll process_name as dropping_process ] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | table firstTime lastTime dest user process_name process_path process_hash parent_process_name sideload_dll sideload_path dropping_process
ucs.kql:
// LuminousMoth — signed loader + sibling DLL side-load from non-install directory
let _signed_loaders = dynamic(["zoom.exe","zoomlauncher.exe","zoomvideoapp.exe","winword.exe","onenote.exe","potplayermini.exe","mqsvc.exe"]);
let _sideload_dlls  = dynamic(["version.dll","wwlib.dll","winnsi.dll","mfc100u.dll","libcef.dll","msi.dll"]);
let _legit_paths    = dynamic([@"\program files\",@"\program files (x86)\",@"\appdata\roaming\zoom\",@"\microsoft office\",@"\windowsapps\"]);
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"          // skip machine accounts
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_signed_loaders)
| where FileName in~ (_sideload_dlls)
| extend _loader_dir = tolower(InitiatingProcessFolderPath), _dll_dir = tolower(FolderPath)
| where _loader_dir == _dll_dir                              // sibling DLL — classic side-load
| where not(_loader_dir has_any (_legit_paths))              // and NOT in vendor install path
| project Timestamp, DeviceName,
          User       = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          Loader     = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          LoaderPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          LoaderSHA256 = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          SideloadDll = FileName,
          DllSHA256   = SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// LuminousMoth — signed loader + sibling DLL side-load (Sysmon EID 7)
let _signed_loaders = dynamic(["zoom.exe","zoomlauncher.exe","zoomvideoapp.exe","winword.exe","onenote.exe","potplayermini.exe","mqsvc.exe"]);
let _sideload_dlls  = dynamic(["version.dll","wwlib.dll","winnsi.dll","mfc100u.dll","libcef.dll","msi.dll"]);
let _legit_paths    = dynamic([@"\program files\",@"\program files (x86)\",@"\appdata\roaming\zoom\",@"\microsoft office\",@"\windowsapps\"]);
WindowsEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 7        // ImageLoaded
| extend Image       = tolower(tostring(EventData.Image)),
         ImageLoaded = tolower(tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded)),
         User        = tostring(EventData.User),
         Signed      = tostring(EventData.Signed)
| extend LoaderName = tostring(split(Image, @"\")[-1]),
         DllName    = tostring(split(ImageLoaded, @"\")[-1])
| extend LoaderDir = strcat_array(array_slice(split(Image, @"\"), 0, -2), @"\"),
         DllDir    = strcat_array(array_slice(split(ImageLoaded, @"\"), 0, -2), @"\")
| where LoaderName in (_signed_loaders)
| where DllName    in (_sideload_dlls)
| where LoaderDir == DllDir                                   // sibling DLL
| where not(LoaderDir has_any (_legit_paths))                 // not the legit install path
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, User, Image, ImageLoaded, Signed
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: LuminousMoth DLL Side-Loading via Signed Loader from Non-Install Path
id: 8b1d4f3a-2c0e-4d3a-9b27-c2a4ef9d10aa
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects the LuminousMoth (G1014) / Mustang Panda execution chain in which a
  legitimately-signed Zoom or Microsoft Office binary launched from a non-
  install directory side-loads a malicious DLL such as version.dll, wwlib.dll
  or winnsi.dll located alongside it.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1014/
  - https://securelist.com/apt-luminousmoth/103332/
  - https://www.welivesecurity.com/2022/03/10/mustang-panda-hodur-old-tricks-new-korplug-variant/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
logsource:
  category: image_load
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_loader:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\Zoom.exe'
      - '\ZoomLauncher.exe'
      - '\ZoomVideoApp.exe'
      - '\winword.exe'
      - '\OneNote.exe'
      - '\PotPlayerMini.exe'
      - '\mqsvc.exe'
  selection_dll:
    ImageLoaded|endswith:
      - '\version.dll'
      - '\wwlib.dll'
      - '\winnsi.dll'
      - '\mfc100u.dll'
      - '\libcef.dll'
  filter_install_paths:
    Image|contains:
      - '\Program Files\'
      - '\Program Files (x86)\'
      - '\AppData\Roaming\Zoom\'
      - '\Microsoft Office\'
      - '\WindowsApps\'
  condition: selection_loader and selection_dll and not filter_install_paths
fields:
  - Image
  - ImageLoaded
  - User
  - Signed
  - Hashes
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate portable installs of Zoom or PotPlayer outside Program Files
  - Software repackaged by IT into ProgramData / custom paths
level: high
tags:
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1574.001
  - attack.t1036.005
  - attack.g1014
  - attack.g0129
ucs.rationale: LuminousMoth/Mustang Panda's defining tradecraft is hijacking a known-good Zoom/Office signed binary by relocating it to ProgramData, %TEMP%, %PUBLIC% or a removable drive root and dropping a malicious sibling DLL (most famously version.dll loaded by ZoomVideoApp.exe and wwlib.dll loaded by winword.exe — see Kaspersky's 2021 'LuminousMoth: Sweeping Attacks for the Chosen Few'). The combination of

ucs.title: [LLM] LuminousMoth USB worm trio — EXE+DLL+LNK dropped to removable drive root
ucs.description: Hunts LuminousMoth's removable-media propagation behaviour where the implant writes its signed-loader EXE, a sideloadable DLL, and a decoy LNK shortcut onto the root of a non-system drive within a short time window — the canonical pattern its USB-spreading component (and Mustang Panda's HIUPAN/PUBLOAD worm) uses to jump air gaps and traverse SE Asian government supply chains.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=false count values(Filesystem.file_name) as files values(Filesystem.file_path) as paths min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action="created" AND (Filesystem.file_name="*.exe" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.lnk") AND NOT (Filesystem.file_path="C:\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="\\\\*") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.process_guid _time span=2m | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | eval exts=mvmap(files, lower(replace(files,"^.*\.",""))) | eval has_exe=if(mvfind(exts,"^exe$")>=0,1,0), has_dll=if(mvfind(exts,"^dll$")>=0,1,0), has_lnk=if(mvfind(exts,"^lnk$")>=0,1,0) | where has_exe=1 AND has_dll=1 AND has_lnk=1 | where mvcount(files) <= 12 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | table firstTime lastTime dest user process_name files paths
ucs.kql:
// LuminousMoth — process drops EXE + DLL + LNK to a non-system drive root within 2 minutes
DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType == "FileCreated"
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
| where FolderPath matches regex @"(?i)^[D-Z]:\\(?:[^\\]+\\)?$"        // drive root or one folder deep on D: – Z:
| extend Ext = tolower(extract(@"\.([^.\\]+)$", 1, FileName))
| where Ext in ("exe","dll","lnk")
| summarize FirstWrite  = min(Timestamp),
            LastWrite   = max(Timestamp),
            Files       = make_set(FileName, 25),
            Exts        = make_set(Ext, 5),
            Drive       = any(FolderPath),
            FileCount   = dcount(FileName)
            by DeviceId, DeviceName,
               InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessSHA256,
               InitiatingProcessAccountName, bin(Timestamp, 2m)
| where Exts has "exe" and Exts has "dll" and Exts has "lnk"            // the trio
| where FileCount <= 12                                                  // worm payload, not bulk copy
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("explorer.exe","setup.exe","msiexec.exe","7zg.exe","winrar.exe","7zfm.exe")
| order by FirstWrite desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// LuminousMoth — EXE+DLL+LNK trio written to non-system drive root via ASIM file events
ImFileEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventType == "FileCreated"
| where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
| where TargetFilePath matches regex @"(?i)^[D-Z]:\\(?:[^\\]+\\)?$"
| extend Ext = tolower(extract(@"\.([^.\\]+)$", 1, TargetFileName))
| where Ext in ("exe","dll","lnk")
| summarize FirstWrite = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastWrite  = max(TimeGenerated),
            Files      = make_set(TargetFileName, 25),
            Exts       = make_set(Ext, 5),
            Drive      = any(TargetFilePath),
            FileCount  = dcount(TargetFileName)
            by DvcHostname, ActingProcessName, ActorUsername, bin(TimeGenerated, 2m)
| where Exts has "exe" and Exts has "dll" and Exts has "lnk"
| where FileCount <= 12
| where ActingProcessName !in~ ("explorer.exe","setup.exe","msiexec.exe","7zg.exe","winrar.exe","7zfm.exe")
| order by FirstWrite desc
ucs.rationale: LuminousMoth's USB-propagation routine (and its Mustang Panda HIUPAN/PUBLOAD/WispRider variants) writes a renamed signed loader EXE, its sideloaded DLL, and a decoy LNK onto the root of removable drives — Kaspersky observed thousands of Myanmar hosts infected this way in 2021. Filtering for the three-extension trio dropped to a non-C drive letter by the same process within a 2-minute window separa
